Container.Name: Marquee
Container.Overview:
Marquee turns a Google Nest Hub into a cinematic Plex now-playing display. When you press play it casts a designed card with the poster, title logo, plot, Rotten Tomatoes and IMDb scores, media details, and live progress; when the credits roll the Hub returns to ambient mode automatically.
Five hand-designed templates (Spotlight, Split, Hero, Lower Third, Big Clock), four themes plus a custom accent color, and a drag-and-drop block editor with instant preview. Cast devices are auto-discovered on your LAN, so setup is: add your Plex token, open the settings page, press Scan, pick your Hub.
Requires Plex Media Server and a Google Cast device (Nest Hub, Chromecast with a screen) on the same LAN. Runs LAN-only with no login and no cloud; your Plex token never leaves the server. Settings page at port 8084.

Config.Name: Nest Hub IP
Config.Description: Optional — leave empty and pick your Hub with Scan on the settings page, or set the Hub's static LAN IP here.

Config.Name: Card URL
Config.Description: URL the Hub loads, so it must be this server's LAN IP — localhost will not work here.

Config.Name: Plex URL
Config.Description: Plex Media Server URL. Keep localhost if Plex runs on this Unraid server; otherwise use its LAN IP.

Config.Name: Plex Token
Config.Description: Plex X-Plex-Token; treat it like a password.

Config.Name: Plex Users
Config.Description: Comma-separated Plex usernames that trigger the marquee. Leave empty to react to everyone streaming from the server, including shared users.

Config.Name: Plex Devices
Config.Description: Comma-separated player/device names that trigger the marquee (as shown on the settings page under Active sessions). Leave empty for any device. Also editable live in settings.
